title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes:
I use winetricks to install allfonts and dotnet472.
Add "PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 %command%" to launch options (without quotes).
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes:
Tricks to make this work: 

1. Turn on MSAA and set lang to english:
edit this file -> <STEAM_HOME>/steamapps/compatdata/359870/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/Square\ Enix/FINAL\ FANTASY\ X\&X-2\ HD\ Remaster/GameSetting.ini

set 
MSAA=2 
Language=en


2. Move launcher in local files. from FFX&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe to FFX&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe-old, then create a simple shortcut which points at FFX.exe,
example: 
 mv FFX\&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe FFX\&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe-old 

ln -s FFX.exe FFX\&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes: Tricks to make this work: 1. Turn on MSAA and set lang to english: edit this file -> <STEAM_HOME>/steamapps/compatdata/359870/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/Square\ Enix/FINAL\ FANTASY\ X\&X-2\ HD\ Remaster/GameSetting.ini set MSAA=2 Language=en 2. Move launcher in local files. from FFX&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe to FFX&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe-old, then create a simple shortcut which points at FFX.exe, example: mv FFX\&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe FFX\&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe-old ln -s FFX.exe FFX\&X-2_LAUNCHER.exe
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes:
First i needed to install a few things to get the launcher and the sound working:
"WINEPREFIX=YOUR PATH HERE/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/359870/pfx/ winetricks --force xact" and
"WINEPREFIX=YOUR PATH HERE/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/359870/pfx/ winetricks --force -q allfonts" and
"WINEPREFIX=YOUR PATH HERE/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/359870/pfx/ winetricks --force -q dotnet40"
After that i used "WINEPREFIX=YOUR PATH HERE/SteamLibrary/steamapps/compatdata/359870/pfx/ winecfg" to set the prefix back to Win7 (All 4 without quotes)
To prevent the game from freezing the parameter "PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%" is needed (again without quotes).
I played for over 2 hours and so far the game is perfectly playable without stutter/glitches whatsoever.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes: With the latest proton the game finally installs and starts (including the launcher) without any protontricks/winetricks used. HOWEVER, it is still unplayable. If you start it without any launch options, the game will quickly freeze on the intro video. Setting "PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command%" as a launch command makes the game run perfectly until you get to the first Yuna summon scene. It just stops and hangs there forever with only the music playing in the background. If you get past that point, the next spot the game freezes will be on the first Sin fight on the boat. X-2 does this right at the start in the first fight. Using "PROTON_USE_WINED3D=1 %command%" as a launch option makes the games unplayable on my system due to horrible blinking and graphical glitches.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes: The game freezes in the intro

title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes:
Unfortunately, this game crashes each time you make a summon. I've tried disabling esync, d3d11 to no avail. In order to get the launcher to run dotnet40 and allfonts had to be installed with winetricks.

I was able to get past some of the aeon cut scenes by using lutris to launch the game, however that will then crash in another random scene. So either I can play through the game by completely ruining the immersion aspect of it by constantly switching and re-watching cut scenes that cannot be skipped only to have a crash later, or I must give up!

Overall, unlplayable for now in my opinion.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes: Freezes during the summon animation. No workarounds made a difference. Outside of that, works flawless so far. But since Aeons are mandatory to the game, it's unplayable.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes: The game seems to run smoothly with 4.11-1 using "PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 PROTON_USE_D9VK=1 %command%", and manually setting MSAA=2 and Language=en inside GameSetting.ini as others have noted for previous versions. No other changes were used. The file in question is at:  <STEAM_HOME>/steamapps/compatdata/359870/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/My\ Documents/Square\ Enix/FINAL\ FANTASY\ X\&X-2\ HD\ Remaster/GameSetting.ini.  Trying to change the settings in the launcher window caused an error, so manually editing the file was still required. But the launcher seems to work just fine otherwise.

title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes: The other mentioned fixes allow the game to start, true, but I still am unable to pass the part when Yuna summons the first Aeon. The camera is still blocked at that point, and no matter what I do, it will not "unlock" and return to the "battle arena". BTW D9VK is not needed as the game uses dxvk as its backed for DX11, anyway (evidenced for those on nVidia by the use of __GL_SHOW_GRAPHICS_OSD=1 environment variable to know which actual API is being used to render the game, so if other means have been used and you see VK in the upper left corner, means that dxvk is being utilized.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes:
Game boots and launcher settings work after following some of the tricks to use from previous reports.  The game menu when pressing ESC shows text but the background is missing (showing white blocks instead) and the indicator showing what setting you are on does not show.  Luckily though the description text is still shown up top of the screen so that gives you an idea of what you are selecting in the menu.  

I have tried using 4.11-1 and results were mostly the same except the text in the menu was gone making it difficult to know where you are in the menu.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes: This is my second report. At first, I had to use all the suggested workarounds and got stuck on the tutorial fight with the aeon summoning. After REMOVING PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE=1 %command% from the start options I got past the fight!
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes:
Initially encountered an infrequent  bug whereby the next turn would not show and would be stuck in an endless battle scene. After removing all launch options, all bugs and glitches seems to have to disappeared. Max settings, stable 30fps (locked to game). FMVs work well and transitions to gameplay smoothly. The only issue is that sound is forced through the controller so have to disable all other output devices with pulse volume audio control before launching.

Currently playing stock game, no changes to launch options, nor have I edited any game folders.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster

notes:
I am updating my report from the one directly below with a bronze rating where I stated I was unable to play the game.  I am near the end game, these are my reports:

First edit the .ini files to set MSAA and change language to english as others have described.

The best solution for framerate  is the launch option:PROTON_FORCE_LARGE_ADDRESS_AWARE =1, however this results in freezing whenever using Valefor, if an Aeon dies, or if using dark/silence attacks from Wakka.  

I ended up completing almost all of the game using:
PROTON_USE_WINED3D11=1 

However this results in some black menus, some rare glitches in game where a character or background is black, and framerate drops.  When the framerate gets slow, just restart the game.  Also, all solutions result in sound not working at the start, just restart the game over and over until it works, sometimes exit the launcher and restart.

I alternated various launch options, primarily these 2 to power through certain areas, but once you get deeper into the game the second option just works.  

Currently at 68 hours working on end game content before beating it.
title: FINAL FANTASY X/X-2 HD Remaster
